verses.text: The psalmist calls on God, 'to whom vengeance belongeth,' to rise up and judge, asking how long the wicked shall triumph. He longs for justice. When evil seems to win, the believer appeals to the righteous Judge.

The psalmist calls on God, 'to whom vengeance belongeth,' to rise up and judge, asking how long the wicked shall triumph. He longs for justice. When evil seems to win, the believer appeals to the righteous Judge.

The psalmist calls on God, 'to whom vengeance belongeth,' to rise up and judge, asking how long the wicked shall triumph. He longs for justice. When evil seems to win, the believer appeals to the righteous Judge.
The wicked boast, oppress, and slay the widow and orphan, saying, 'The LORD shall not see.' They presume on His silence. The cruelty that imagines God blind only deepens its own guilt.

'He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? he that formed the eye, shall he not see?' He rebukes their folly. The God who gave us senses surely perceives all we do and think.
'He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? he that formed the eye, shall he not see?' He rebukes their folly. The God who gave us senses surely perceives all we do and think.

'Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O LORD, and teachest... out of thy law,' giving him rest from evil days. Discipline is a mercy. God's correction, joined to His word, is a kindness to the soul.

'Blessed is the man whom thou chastenest, O LORD, and teachest... out of thy law,' giving him rest from evil days. Discipline is a mercy. God's correction, joined to His word, is a kindness to the soul.
'The LORD will not cast off his people,' for judgment shall return to righteousness and the upright follow it. He keeps His own. Though justice seems delayed, it will surely be restored.
'The LORD will not cast off his people,' for judgment shall return to righteousness and the upright follow it. He keeps His own. Though justice seems delayed, it will surely be restored.

Who would stand for him against evildoers but the LORD, his help and refuge? 'In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.' God steadies him. When anxious thoughts crowd in, God's comforts bring the soul delight.

Who would stand for him against evildoers but the LORD, his help and refuge? 'In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.' God steadies him. When anxious thoughts crowd in, God's comforts bring the soul delight.

Can corrupt rulers, who frame mischief by law, have fellowship with God? No; the LORD is his defence and 'shall bring upon them their own iniquity.' Wickedness recoils on itself. God will repay the unjust with the evil they devised.
